title: Threat Intelligence & Reconnaissance
overview: Your first module at NEXUS establishes your security workstation and teaches reconnaissance methodology. You'll learn what attackers can discover about an organization without ever touching their systems — then apply those skills to a real client assessment.

units_breakdown.labDesc: Build your security workstation. Install VirtualBox (or Parallels on Mac), Kali Linux, and verify your toolset.
Master Nmap, Netcat, and OSINT tools. Practice on training targets before going live.
Client engagement. External reconnaissance assessment + professional Engagement Packet.

clientContext.label: Client Context: Brazos Financial Group
clientContext.body: Ray Jimenez (CTO) wants to understand what an attacker could discover about Brazos without insider access. The SEC examination is in 6 months — he needs ammunition for his security budget presentation to the board.

stakeholders.name: Ray Jimenez
stakeholders.title: CTO, Brazos Financial Group
stakeholders.concern: I need ammunition for my budget presentation to the board. Find what an attacker could discover about us without insider access — the more concerning, the stronger my case.

footer: Start with Lab 1.1 in the Modules section.
